miniQuest.name: Collect Stackpeak Eggs
miniQuest.description: §7Bring §3[60 Stackpeak Eggs]§7 to the Slaying Post at §f[-1675, 40, -1624]

worldEvent.name: Steel Skirmish
worldEvent.description: §7A massive presence of military might is required to keep watch over such a sprawling cityscape as the Industrial District. 󐀀§f󐀂󐀂 §a§lShift Right-Click To Fast Travel

secretDiscovery.name: A Deathly Warning
secretDiscovery.description: §7Atop of the Tower of Ascension stands a being who claims to be Death himself. While such claims are a lie, it can't be ignored that the imposter has horrendous amounts of power, from an unknown source.

secretDiscovery.name: A Dividing Force
secretDiscovery.description: §7The river dividing the two forests has acted as a barrier against the Decay for decades. Using her powers, Lari has single handedly kept the Decay from spreading further south.

secretDiscovery.name: A Hero's Origin
secretDiscovery.description: §7In the year 854 AP (After Portal), a massive horde of undead lead by Bak'al attacked Ragni. The people of the city sought refuge in the sewer, the only safe place left.

secretDiscovery.name: A Monument of Hope
secretDiscovery.description: §7Just as the darkness infiltrated Gavel, the Light was able to protect the physical bodies of the miners in Lutho by erecting this Monolith.

secretDiscovery.name: A Wynnic Excavation
secretDiscovery.description: §7The miners soon discovered there were more than emeralds in the walls of this mountain range - Something from out of this world.

secretDiscovery.name: Abandoned Excavations
secretDiscovery.description: §7The drive for emeralds among the Villagers was so great, they excavated nearly all currency in the province. The search for more led the Villagers to explore beyond the sea.

secretDiscovery.name: Ahmsord's Origins
secretDiscovery.description: §7Much like their other outposts, the Skyraiders were forced to abandon their strongest defence after pressure from Villager forces.

secretDiscovery.name: At the Edge of Decay
secretDiscovery.description: §7Suffering from the cryptic Decay, Northern Gavel is slowly withering away. Its ominous downfall has caused the Villagers of Llevigar to become increasingly superstitious.

secretDiscovery.name: Bak'al's Destruction 1
secretDiscovery.description: §7The corrupt entity known as Bak'al has appeared countless times since the portal was opened, decimating everything in his path.

secretDiscovery.name: Bak'al's Destruction 2
secretDiscovery.description: §7A once great city of Akias was reduced to rubble in 347 AP (After Portal) in one of the largest attacks by Bak'al ever documented. There was only one survivor.

secretDiscovery.name: Bak'al's Destruction 3
secretDiscovery.description: §7Just the name of Bak'al is enough to make many cower in fear. They say that wherever he appears, he will only leave death in his wake.

secretDiscovery.name: Beneath the Roots
secretDiscovery.description: §7This cavern is evidence that corruption is like a weed. The roots spread throughout the province.

secretDiscovery.name: Birth of Efilim
secretDiscovery.description: §7No one breaks the utmost laws of Aldorei without exilement. Those who fall in the eyes of the High Elves are banished, never to return. Outcasted from their land, they were forced to find a new home.

secretDiscovery.name: Boulder Breaker
secretDiscovery.description: §7Attacks from the portal were often so brutal, it forced people underground. Sometimes, they never made it back out.

secretDiscovery.name: Boulder Pit
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Emperor's 26th son was named Hashr. As he grew older, he became wrought with greed and envy of his father's power. It wasn't long before he was willing to kill.

secretDiscovery.name: Canyon Housing
secretDiscovery.description: §7Although the canyon tends to shift and destroy the livelihoods of those who live here, it is not enough to hold back the refugees of the dark forests, preferring the canyon's instability to the Decay.

secretDiscovery.name: Chained Goliaths
secretDiscovery.description: §7Because of their immense power, dragons were captured by dwarves and forced to do their bidding. Currently there is only one known living dragon remaining.

secretDiscovery.name: Colossal Ruins
secretDiscovery.description: §7Left behind by the Fracturing, this intriguing ruin is thought to have been part of a second colossus - though it is currently unknown how this one came to be destroyed.

secretDiscovery.name: Cosmic Crystals
secretDiscovery.description: §7The origins of the colorful crystals is well known, though the power that they hold and their purpose is a mystery that has driven many to the brink of insanity.

secretDiscovery.name: Cracked Canyon
secretDiscovery.description: §7Earthquakes form cracks bursting with mystical energy throughout the canyon. These cracks grow with each quake, and eventually, may lead to a catastrophe.

secretDiscovery.name: Crate Crane
secretDiscovery.description: §7Nemract was one of the cities attacked most often. The people would hide in the gutters and sewers until it was over. However, corrupt enemies are not without intelligence, and they were quickly discovered by the enemy.

secretDiscovery.name: Cultural Artefact
secretDiscovery.description: §7Dogun culture flourished before the arrival of the Dwarves. The creators of this massive tablet detailed the history of this most ancient race.

secretDiscovery.name: Decaying Mountains
secretDiscovery.description: §7Creatures residing in mountain caves were forced to search for safer homes down below, after the Decay reached their habitats.

secretDiscovery.name: Desert Royal Residence
secretDiscovery.description: §7When the sceptre was taken from its master, a curse of sandstorms plagued the empire for years. Nearly everything was buried, even the royal palace.

secretDiscovery.name: Desolate Temples
secretDiscovery.description: §7These temples stand as the last remaining pieces of a native civilization within the Sky Islands, centered around the worship of an ancient, colossal being.

secretDiscovery.name: Empty Vaults
secretDiscovery.description: §7Unlike the vast emerald hoards in bank vaults throughout Gavel, Olux's vault has long been empty in its attempt to deal with the Decay.

secretDiscovery.name: Explorer's Corruption Study
secretDiscovery.description: §7Many years ago, a scholar studied how effective Theorick's ice had been so at fighting corruption. Even through the sheer power of the Twains, however, the ice merely slows corruption.

secretDiscovery.name: Far Above the Clouds
secretDiscovery.description: §7In times before the Fracturing, stories told of a great wise beast who roamed the lands. As the Ahms region fell, so too did the dragon leave Gavel forever.

secretDiscovery.name: Far From the Roots
secretDiscovery.description: §7Corruption crawls where one can not see. The darkness; the depths; the mind.

secretDiscovery.name: Fate of the Olm
secretDiscovery.description: §7Many years ago an Olmic oracle foresaw the demise of her land. To prepare for it, she created a weapon of pure light that will halt the darkness, but when the time came to use it, her descendants were unable to retrieve the bracelet.

secretDiscovery.name: Fracture in Reality
secretDiscovery.description: §7When the runes were combined ages ago, the Forgery burst from the ground. Sealed by its anchors is a mysterious fracture in space and time from which the structure draws its power.

secretDiscovery.name: Frozen Cave Hideout
secretDiscovery.description: §7Families like these used to hide underground during raids. In the final raid of Nesaak, Theorick froze the entire place over - freezing them with it.

secretDiscovery.name: Gavel Excavation
secretDiscovery.description: §7Upon discovering the meteors below Lake Gylia, a company dedicated to their collection and observation was established. Eventually, however, their focus shifted towards Wynn.

secretDiscovery.name: Gylia Watch Library
secretDiscovery.description: §7These are the only remaining records that describe the events of the Gerten War. However, history was written by the victor.

secretDiscovery.name: Gylia's Cataclysm
secretDiscovery.description: §7More than two thousand years ago, two meteors crashed down into the Gylia Plains. Over centuries, the crater filled to become a lake where the most curious magic circulates.

secretDiscovery.name: Hallowed Ground
secretDiscovery.description: §7The mythical waters of the Lazarus Pit affect corpses in different ways. Some resurrect with part of their memories intact. Some return as nothing but husks of their former selves.

secretDiscovery.name: Harmless Worms
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Grootslangs are peaceful creatures who live deep underground. It was long thought they were extinct as they have not been seen in centuries. Recently, something has driven them upwards.

secretDiscovery.name: Headless, Not Homeless
secretDiscovery.description: §7Shortly after his revival, Dullahan set his sights on the manor of a wealthy Villager, slaughtering everyone in his sight. To this day, the castle remains under his control.

secretDiscovery.name: Historical Maltic
secretDiscovery.description: §7Maltic was the first settlement ever made by the arriving villagers. The alliance between Villagers and Humans would go on to save the province.

secretDiscovery.name: Light Mushrooms
secretDiscovery.description: §7These strange mushrooms spread across the Light Forest are enveloped in mystery. Their forms are incredibly interchangeable, and their effects on people are even more enigmatic.

secretDiscovery.name: Llevigar's Last Hope
secretDiscovery.description: §7For centuries Gavel was a peaceful place. As time has gone on, increasingly devastating events have plagued the province. Out of a need to survive and expand, the Villagers looked elsewhere for hope...

secretDiscovery.name: Llevigar's Secret Library
secretDiscovery.description: §7This mystical library holds Llevigar's most fascinating, and dangerous books. From guides on cosmic magic to wise dragons of old, this is certainly a place to spend many hours inside.

secretDiscovery.name: Loot of the Lost
secretDiscovery.description: §7Many lose their lives to the canyon, yet their belongings are never to be found. A certain group of hobgoblins collect and hoard all that they can find.

secretDiscovery.name: Lost Avos City
secretDiscovery.description: §7The history of the Avos has remained largely unknown, but this monument of Avos ancestry might hold the ancient secrets that historians have been looking for.

secretDiscovery.name: Lost Farmers
secretDiscovery.description: §7Unable to travel safely, farmers in Olux generally live a lonely life. Few people noticed when they began to disappear.

secretDiscovery.name: Lusuco's Secret Library
secretDiscovery.description: §7This old library has been slumbering beneath Lusuco for years. It tells the story of the legendary Twain children.

secretDiscovery.name: Maex Black Market
secretDiscovery.description: §7Dwarven society is not all it's cracked up to be. In this sleazy black market, every product here is illegal in some way, though most of these criminals are only trying to survive royal austerity.

secretDiscovery.name: Mark of the Meteors
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Crashing of the Sky, as they called it, impacted the lives of Gylia in many ways - Even after centuries of its occurrence.

secretDiscovery.name: Messengers From Beyond
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Light has endless ways to communicate with the inhabitants of Gavel. One such way is through physical beings, from a higher level of existence.

secretDiscovery.name: Mining Cessation
secretDiscovery.description: §7Simple emerald mining turned to disaster as they broke through into the Silent Expanse. In a final act to protect the province, they barricaded themselves in, and sealed their fate.

secretDiscovery.name: Mining Operation
secretDiscovery.description: §7The mysteries uncovered from this excavation were quickly abandoned as an ancient dark force, one far more dangerous than anything this province has encountered, was found to be lurking beyond Wynn's southern border.

secretDiscovery.name: Ne du Valeos du Ellach
secretDiscovery.description: §7Before time, it began. Through the ages it ravaged the lands. Another day, another night, it continues in the worlds beyond. Forever it shall last.

secretDiscovery.name: Necklace Chamber
secretDiscovery.description: §7Long ago, the Avos hid away a powerful necklace. Despite the many tales and legends of it, it has never been located until now.

secretDiscovery.name: Nilrem's Sealed Letter
secretDiscovery.description: §7An ancient letter addressed to Bob's friend Nilrem regarding a dark power...

secretDiscovery.name: Otherworldly Occurrence
secretDiscovery.description: §7The connection between our world and the other realms has been shattered and warped as the war of corruption evolved.

secretDiscovery.name: Otherworldly Origins
secretDiscovery.description: §7Despite being a popular household pet, no one really knows the true origins of Wybels. Some theorize they fell from space, but such claims are often dismissed as conspiracy theories.

secretDiscovery.name: Parasite Cocoon
secretDiscovery.description: §7After a battle with a powerful Elf, the creature that broke into this realm had to recoup its strength. It therefore hibernated in an unknown underground location for many years...

secretDiscovery.name: Pottery Wheel
secretDiscovery.description: §7Theorick was the principal defender of the Nesaak region for many years. The inhabitants volunteered contributions to him, sometimes in gratitude, sometimes in fear. He also loved to make pottery.

secretDiscovery.name: Quicksand
secretDiscovery.description: §7The civilization that ruled this land over 1500 years ago seemed to be equally as educated as people today. There are lots of texts dedicated to the Emperor.

secretDiscovery.name: Ragni's Secret Library
secretDiscovery.description: §7A twelve-volume story of Wynn's great hero can be found here. The origins of this library, and others like it, still remain unknown.

secretDiscovery.name: Relos' Secret Library
secretDiscovery.description: §7This library tells a tale that the government of Corkus has kept secret. Rumors tell that more mysterious libraries like this one are hidden across the world.

secretDiscovery.name: Ruins of Detlas
secretDiscovery.description: §7The modern city of Detlas was built with the help of villagers after their arrival in Wynn. But these ruins might hold the secrets of what lied before...

secretDiscovery.name: Rulers of the Skies
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Skyraiders were known for their domination over the Sky Islands. As the Villagers adapted to the land, the Skyraiders' dominion faded.

secretDiscovery.name: Rymek Thief
secretDiscovery.description: §7After being kicked out of the Wynn cities during the Corruption War, a growing number of bandits began to reassimilate in the desert and mesa.

secretDiscovery.name: Sealed Away
secretDiscovery.description: §7Whatever lies beyond the barrier of the Great Stump has long been forgotten. Any attempts to enter it have been made with no success.

secretDiscovery.name: Sealed in Ice
secretDiscovery.description: §7A scar of the Dogun War. The freezing of this Dogun city left most of its old residents sealed with it, to be forgotten.

secretDiscovery.name: Shadowy Dealings
secretDiscovery.description: §7The highly valued guard golems are quite effective in repelling danger, but they are not enough to help the citizens of Olux. Desperation is the only choice for many.

secretDiscovery.name: Shrouded Lives
secretDiscovery.description: §7When those who worked at the secretive factory returned home, none would speak of their experiences inside of it.

secretDiscovery.name: Silent Expanse's Secret
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Silent Expanse has acted as the gateway to Dern since the outbreak of the Corruption War. The Eye has watched all intruders... and all attempts at escape.

secretDiscovery.name: Somewhere In Between
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Pigmen are a mysterious creature, neither pig nor man, neither corrupt nor whole.

secretDiscovery.name: Space Between Realities
secretDiscovery.description: §7Between Realms of physicality and influence lies the vast nothingness of the void. Some islands have broken from the lands and drift in this space, seeking purpose.

secretDiscovery.name: Spider Ruins
secretDiscovery.description: §7Many inexplicable ancient temples are scattered throughout Gavel, but some of these ruins have been wiped out by the Villagers as they sought to expand. Only a few remain to this day.

secretDiscovery.name: Subterranean Creation
secretDiscovery.description: §7Long ago, the lower levels of Molten Heights were a mere cave system. Using their ancient magic, the Doguns carved out these caves into vast structures.

secretDiscovery.name: Summoner's Fate
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Decay was bad news for the citizens of Gavel. However, it became a haven for amateur warlocks to practice dark magic... poorly.

secretDiscovery.name: Teachings of Old
secretDiscovery.description: §7The history and culture of the Villagers were shaped by magic. Their unusual ways of converting such power to everyday chores was common, but some of these learnings have now been long forgotten by the public.

secretDiscovery.name: Temple Ruin
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Emperor's Sceptre is a mysterious artifact that gave the holder unquestionable power. The people of the Desert Empire constructed temples to honor it.

secretDiscovery.name: Temple of Light
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Elves attempted to convince the Villagers to worship the light. The religion never took hold and thus these temples were abandoned.

secretDiscovery.name: Temple of Swords
secretDiscovery.description: §7After realizing its true power, Hashr realized he must hide the sceptre where no one could find it. Many years later, a group of bandits found refuge in this very temple.

secretDiscovery.name: Thanos Armory
secretDiscovery.description: §7Hidden in the depths of Thanos is a legendary forge. This armoury is where weapons of dragon slaying potential were forged; however, they are no longer to be seen within it.

secretDiscovery.name: The Ancient Ones
secretDiscovery.description: §7These ancient beings are the enigma of Gylia Plains. Their strange structure has deterred many historians from finding the answer to one question - were they beast or stone?

secretDiscovery.name: The Bane of Gavel
secretDiscovery.description: §7Below the forests, far from the eyes of Villagers, lies the terrible truth of the Decay...

secretDiscovery.name: The Broken Protector
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Canyon Colossus wasn't always a threat to its environment. But as the years went on, something seems to have sent the powerful mechanisms of this ancient being haywire.

secretDiscovery.name: The Doguns' Defeat
secretDiscovery.description: §7For years the Doguns fought for their land, but eventually every war must have a victor, although no one is a winner.

secretDiscovery.name: The Fallen Protector
secretDiscovery.description: §7Long ago, a Colossus had once inhabited the Ahms Region before its fracturing. With nobody to keep the being's mechanisms in check, it eventually went haywire and shattered the entire region.

secretDiscovery.name: The Final Moments
secretDiscovery.description: §7Shortly after the portal broke into this hidden land, a beast from an unknown realm laid siege to the city forcing the Olm to its domain.

secretDiscovery.name: The Forest has Eyes
secretDiscovery.description: §7For every miner in Lutho that lost contact with their inner self, two eyes grow from the trees.

secretDiscovery.name: The Grand Archive
secretDiscovery.description: §7This magnificent library holds some of the oldest pieces of literature known to the world. However, not all scriptures have been archived in these halls...

secretDiscovery.name: The Great Barrier
secretDiscovery.description: §7The parasitic creatures are unable to invade the canyon due to a strange force that keeps the mountains in an endless motion.

secretDiscovery.name: The Heart of the Decay
secretDiscovery.description: §7For two hundred years the nest of the parasitic creatures remained hidden. It released countless Dark leeches into the undergrowth of the province. By the time it was found, it was already too late.

secretDiscovery.name: The Lone Telescope
secretDiscovery.description: §7The large crystal floating above the Tower of Ascension was not of earthly origins. While powerful, the strange magical aura it exudes has caused various strange happenings in the surrounding region.

secretDiscovery.name: The Lonely Abyss
secretDiscovery.description: §7The Void is a limitless expanse transcending the concepts of space, time, and reality itself. Those who are stranded within its abyss are left to wander its boundless depths for eternity.

secretDiscovery.name: The Talor Crypt
secretDiscovery.description: §7Crypts such as these were commonly used to bury the richest in the area. However, the Decay's aggressive nature resulted in these crypts becoming overfilled with corpses, and eventually abandoned.

secretDiscovery.name: The Twains' Downfall
secretDiscovery.description: §7With the death of Marius Twain, the family fell apart, leaving Wynn's protectors alone... or worse.

secretDiscovery.name: Timeless Ruin
secretDiscovery.description: §7The people who built these mysterious structures had unusual control over time magic. Very few alive understand it or know about the people who lived here, for they were neither Human nor Villager. A species that fled the plains thousands of years ago.

secretDiscovery.name: Tomb of the Founders
secretDiscovery.description: §7Commanding the battalions of Molten Heights to victory, these two revered Dwarven leaders' likeness inspire all those who enter through this gate.

secretDiscovery.name: Torn in Twain
secretDiscovery.description: §7Of the Twains, Theorick had a different idea of how defending the province should look and the rights that gave the defender. Eventually these differences lead to him breaking off from the family to found his own land.

secretDiscovery.name: Totem Pole
secretDiscovery.description: §7Following the death of the Emperor, sandstorms raged across the land, and the Eagle Tribe was forced to take refuge in an abandoned prayer cave.

secretDiscovery.name: Toxic Bounce
secretDiscovery.description: §7The land influenced by Dern can manifest in many ways, here a monster and landscape merged to become one sentient creature.

secretDiscovery.name: Unstable Experiments
secretDiscovery.description: §7Quartz's abilities of absorbing energy have been experimented on by various scientists throughout the years. Although its potential is great, it becomes increasingly unstable when used.

secretDiscovery.name: Watchmen
secretDiscovery.description: §7From the watch towers set up across the province, scouts were able to spot an incoming horde from miles away, helping to prepare for countless battles.

secretDiscovery.name: Water of the Past
secretDiscovery.description: §7The proud farmers surrounding Ragni were often left to fend for themselves. They have proven to be resourceful and hardened. They created their own infrastructure, but when they were totally abandoned by the army, there was nothing but slaughter.

secretDiscovery.name: Ways of the Wicked
secretDiscovery.description: §7Kander may appear to be nothing but a dying forest filled with superstitious inhabitants, but something far more sinister has lurked beneath its repulsive veil.

secretDiscovery.name: Wolf Temple
secretDiscovery.description: §7This monument was built in memory of Bob, who supposedly fell to wild wolves here. Those who knew him have their doubts, however, and the origins of the mysterious temples and tombs are unknown.

secretDiscovery.name: Wrecking Ball
secretDiscovery.description: §7When the Creden Tibus discovered the Emperor's Sceptre in an ancient, hidden temple, they decided to hide it closer to home. After many generations, they grew paranoid, and moved it once more.

secretDiscovery.name: Wynn Plains Monument
secretDiscovery.description: §7This ancient monument only opens to those it deems worthy. Inside is contained secrets of the Wynn Plains that few eyes have ever seen.

worldDiscovery.description: §7This recluse gap in reality has laid unknown to all but a few for an immeasurable amount of time. Within its walls lies a colossal hole in the barrier between realms, and within that hole lies an anomalous, otherworldly entity responsible for the creation of the Void Holes that scatter the Silent Expanse.

worldDiscovery.name: Abandoned Lumber Yard
worldDiscovery.description: §7Hundreds of years ago, the people working at this mill died in the war. Even after the land was frozen over, their bodies still hunt those who draw near.

worldDiscovery.name: Abandoned Mines
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite mining opening up the infamous portal, it did not stop. Some speculate that these mines are hiding an even greater danger...

worldDiscovery.name: Aelumia Citadel
worldDiscovery.description: §7Fruma's shining capital, Aelumia Citadel, stands strong atop the central plateau, overlooking the entire province. Though citizens enjoy a life free of oppression, the paranoid government keeps rigid surveillance systems to ensure nobody steps too far out of line.

worldDiscovery.name: Aerie of the Recluse
worldDiscovery.description: §7This lush, open-air cavern is home to a secluded pair of Avos. They have some kind of vendetta against humans... it may be best to leave them be.

worldDiscovery.name: Ahmsord
worldDiscovery.description: §7The central city of the Sky Islands, Ahmsord is home to the wealthiest of traders, and the most revered of adventurers.

worldDiscovery.name: Aldorei Town
worldDiscovery.description: §7Aldorei Town is another section of the elf city, housing many special resources and charms not found anywhere else in Gavel.

worldDiscovery.name: Aldorei Valley
worldDiscovery.description: §7Very few outsiders are allowed into Aldorei Valley, home of the elves. They consider it to be a place free of greed.

worldDiscovery.name: Aldwell
worldDiscovery.description: §7Though this community seems warm and welcoming, something isn't quite right. An air of paranoia is constantly present.

worldDiscovery.name: Alekin Village
worldDiscovery.description: §7A small village hidden within the trees in Nivla Woods. It acts as a respite for travelers, as the midpoint between Ragni and Detlas.

worldDiscovery.name: Almuj
worldDiscovery.description: §7Even in the harsh desert, people have found a way to survive. The massive city of Almuj uses the nearby water sources to keep its city a thriving oasis.

worldDiscovery.name: Almuj Great River
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite its barren surroundings, this river has sprouted various plant life. It flows straight into the Rymek Canyon.

worldDiscovery.name: Altar of Sanctification
worldDiscovery.description: §7A heavy aura hangs in the air of this disused tribal exorcism site. The gibbering of a lingering spirit has begun to echo in your ears...

worldDiscovery.name: Ancient Nemract
worldDiscovery.description: §7Hundreds of years ago, the original stone city of Nemract was ravaged by a swarm of the undead. Now it is only inhabited by the corpses of those who died that day.

worldDiscovery.name: Arena of the Legends
worldDiscovery.description: §7An arena for those who wish to truly test their mettle after being admitted into the Temple of the Legends, where fierce training takes place.

worldDiscovery.name: Auburn Forest
worldDiscovery.description: §7A shortage of water has caused this forest to exist in an eternal autumn. The poorest region in Fruma, its inhabitants manage an economy of their own based on bartering, allowing them a better quality of life.

worldDiscovery.name: Avos Territory
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home of the bird-like Avos, the native inhabitants of Corkus. Though they welcomed the Corkians initially, tensions between the two have grown over the years.

worldDiscovery.name: Bantisu Air Temple
worldDiscovery.description: §7Bantisu Air Temple has served as a beacon in the Canyon of The Lost. The Air Monks help guide people who have gotten lost.

worldDiscovery.name: Bear Zoo
worldDiscovery.description: §7This strange island is home to the province's one and only zoo. The owner thought out in the ocean the creatures would be safe from the corruption, but the zoo doesn't get many visitors.

worldDiscovery.name: Black Road
worldDiscovery.description: §7This road leads to the town of Ternaves and its nearby mine. It is inhabited by strange mushroom-cow hybrids, and a mysterious scientist.

worldDiscovery.name: Bob's Tomb
worldDiscovery.description: §7A year after Bob's disappearance, a tomb pertaining to the resting place of Bob was found in 985 AP.

worldDiscovery.name: Bottomless Pit
worldDiscovery.description: §7An intense well of gravity sits atop the border of the void, drawing in anything fallen into it. Creatures seem to still live down here...

worldDiscovery.name: Bovine Barn
worldDiscovery.description: §7A lush cavern hidden under the barn at Durum Isles. It is bursting with life and natural energies, and is fiercely protected...

worldDiscovery.name: Bremminglar
worldDiscovery.description: §7Not many travel to this little tribal town in the savannah, but it is said to be the home of a powerful sorcerer.

worldDiscovery.name: Bremmyn-Hierra
worldDiscovery.description: §7Sandblasted by desert winds, the ancient mountain of Bremmyn-Hierra stands cracked but tall above the savanna plains, a guiding mark for many.

worldDiscovery.name: Bucie
worldDiscovery.description: §7The small town of Bucie is home to many farmers, who produce the crops for the nearby cities of Llevigar and Olux.

worldDiscovery.name: Caritat Mansion
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once the home of the Caritat family, the most wealthy and generous family in the forest, until one day they mysteriously vanished.

worldDiscovery.name: Carnivorous Creature
worldDiscovery.description: §7This ravenous plant is truly one of a kind. Many creatures have lived hundreds of years beyond their natural lifespan due to the influence of darkness.

worldDiscovery.name: Castle Dullahan
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once the home of a wealthy aristocrat, this castle has been overtaken by Dullahan, an enigmatic Elf who terrorized the Dark Forest for decades.

worldDiscovery.name: Centerworld Fortress
worldDiscovery.description: §7The largest Orc base in Gavel, this fortress was once considered the heart of Orcish territory. It still serves as a capital, and is strictly defended.

worldDiscovery.name: Challenge of the Blades
worldDiscovery.description: §7Pure dark magic is all that exists in this magical trap, laid by a sadistic denizen from the Realm of Darkness.

worldDiscovery.name: Cinfras
worldDiscovery.description: §7The city of Cinfras is the central hub of the Gavel Province. It is also home of the massive Guild Hall, and a massive airship travel center.

worldDiscovery.name: Corkus City
worldDiscovery.description: §7The main city of the patriotic Corkians, refugees from Fruma who settled on the island. They've used the art of electromagic to build machines.

worldDiscovery.name: Crystal Falls
worldDiscovery.description: §7The elegant Crystal Falls decorate the scenery around the dojo of Sensei Miyagi, a wise teacher who has trained hundreds of apprentices.

worldDiscovery.name: Dark Forest Gate
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once a grand stone entryway to a brilliant forest, it has since fallen into disrepair since the emergence of the Decay.

worldDiscovery.name: Dark Obelisk
worldDiscovery.description: §7This black obelisk bears a similar shape to the one found in Lutho, though it's unclear how they're related.

worldDiscovery.name: Dead Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7A wealthy family claimed the entire island to settle on when the waves first settled, but a mysterious event has brought dark influence to the island, robbing it of its vibrancy.

worldDiscovery.name: Death's Realm
worldDiscovery.description: §7Devoid of color, all the souls of the dead will make their way to here, the realm of the dead.

worldDiscovery.name: Delnar Manor
worldDiscovery.description: §7A prestigious mansion that was once owned by the Sodeta Guild. It is said that the dangerous and powerful Abysso Galoshes were once sealed away here.

worldDiscovery.name: Deserter's Refuge
worldDiscovery.description: §7The lavish halls of these barracks are one of the few places in the Citadel that the lighthouse's piercing gaze cannot reach inside of. As only soldiers are permitted to enter, none have heard the maniacal whisperings that now fill them.

worldDiscovery.name: Detlas
worldDiscovery.description: §7The center of the Wynn Province. This city is a massive hub of trade for all adventurers, whether they have just started their journey or have been at it their whole lives.

worldDiscovery.name: Detlas Suburbs
worldDiscovery.description: §7This area is heavily defended by the armies of Detlas and Ragni to ensure food production continues.

worldDiscovery.name: Dogun's Retreat
worldDiscovery.description: §7The tiny survivor population of Doguns fled to the surface after the subterranean war. They are ill suited to living above ground.

worldDiscovery.name: Dragon Skeleton
worldDiscovery.description: §7Remnant of the Dogun genocide, the Dragons sided with the native residents. However, the dwarves proved to be the dominant force beneath the lava.

worldDiscovery.name: Durum Isles
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite the odd formation of land, every inch is covered in farmland, and acts as a proverbial breadbasket for the ocean capital of Selchar.

worldDiscovery.name: Eagle Tribe
worldDiscovery.description: §7The Eagle Tribe is one of the two native tribes of the Rymek Canyon. They have been at war with the Owl Tribe for years.

worldDiscovery.name: Efilim
worldDiscovery.description: §7Unlike most towns in Gavel, Efilim is home to multiple races. Elves and villagers live together in harmony here.

worldDiscovery.name: Elkurn
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite its proximity to the portal, this quaint town has somehow managed to survive. Its placement near two separate magical phenomena has led to an influx of scientific minds to the area.

worldDiscovery.name: Eltom
worldDiscovery.description: §7The city of Eltom is the only human residence in Gavel. Since humans aren't permitted to settle in the province, it has remained hidden to outsiders.

worldDiscovery.name: Emerald Trail
worldDiscovery.description: §7The trail through Nivla Woods to Detlas, the central city of the Wynn Province.

worldDiscovery.name: Entamis Village
worldDiscovery.description: §7A native tribe of the Dernel Jungle resides here. Its people revere the tree in the village's center as their guardian.

worldDiscovery.name: Espren
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite being Fruma's poorest city, the people of Espren get by through their strong sense of community, and faith in their spiritual guardians.

worldDiscovery.name: Eyeball Forest
worldDiscovery.description: §7The trees of this forest are unlike any seen in the rest of the Wynn Province, and give many travellers the feeling that they're being watched...

worldDiscovery.name: Fallen Factory
worldDiscovery.description: §7This massive factory, built by the engineer Cerid, was once hailed as the national monument of Corkus. Ever since it was closed down, it has fallen into disrepair.

worldDiscovery.name: Faltach Manor
worldDiscovery.description: §7No one ever dares to go near this abandoned manor, even though only a few know of its shrouded past. Some have gone as far to say that they can hear something... or someone... calling to them from within.

worldDiscovery.name: Feuding Houses
worldDiscovery.description: §7The noble houses Nasin and Thalas have long held great influence over the province, bearing many of Fruma's past Sovereigns. For hundreds of years, they have maintained a bitter rivalry, always attempting to one-up each other.

worldDiscovery.name: Fort Hegea
worldDiscovery.description: §7While most Military activity in the Mistwoods takes place in the outskirts of the forest, this fort is an anomaly, hidden in the deepest reaches of the region. One can only wonder what clandestine activity would require something so covert.

worldDiscovery.name: Fort Tericen
worldDiscovery.description: §7Fort Tericen is one of Fruma's most important military fortresses, being home to the province's largest military academy. Recruits are brought from all across the province to be trained here.

worldDiscovery.name: Fort Torann
worldDiscovery.description: §7Behind the Royal Gate lies the largest military fortress in Fruma, Fort Torann. It is built to protect the province from intruders, and all who enter or leave must pass through it.

worldDiscovery.name: Fountain of Youth
worldDiscovery.description: §7It is said that each province has a magical spring that grants people eternal life.

worldDiscovery.name: Freezing Heights
worldDiscovery.description: §7Cursed by an ice dragon's breath, this is a scar of the Dogun genocide.

worldDiscovery.name: Gavel Gate
worldDiscovery.description: §7This massive doorway serves as the main entrance to the province of Gavel. Through it lies the quartz city of Llevigar.

worldDiscovery.name: Gelibord
worldDiscovery.description: §7Despite the Decay, Gelibord has gotten by. Due to its trade industry, it's become a good stop for adventurers on their quest through Gavel.

worldDiscovery.name: Gert Camp
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home of the Gerts, a race of hunger fuelled orange-headed brutes. Though they aren't very intelligent, they are still a force to be reckoned with.

worldDiscovery.name: Geyser Pit
worldDiscovery.description: §7Desperate for resources, the mining mechs of Corkus have begun digging to bedrock. The magma layers aren't far below.

worldDiscovery.name: Gylia Watch Tower
worldDiscovery.description: §7Founded after the Gerten War 200 years ago, the Gylia Watch have used this tower as their base, keeping an eye on the Gerts to prevent another attack.

worldDiscovery.name: Half-Moon Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7This island is home to some form of cult, obsessed with the moon and its power. Rumours fly about it, a common thought being that they are attempting to summon some form of lunar deity.

worldDiscovery.name: House of Talor
worldDiscovery.description: §7This once grand cathedral has begun to fall into disrepair over the years as the effects of the Decay worsened. Nevertheless, the church still continues to provide blessings and protection to the Kander Forest's inhabitants.

worldDiscovery.name: Hyloch
worldDiscovery.description: §7Those who are not rich enough to afford their own mansions live here in Hyloch, by the vast lakes. Its people live in bliss, ignorant to the suffering of their countrymen in the lower regions.

worldDiscovery.name: Iboju Village
worldDiscovery.description: §7The village of the native tribe of the jungle. The people here have lived peacefully alongside the people of Troms for years.

worldDiscovery.name: Ice Islands
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home to a nation of early seafarers, they have waged war among each other for years, oblivious to the real war happening on the mainland.

worldDiscovery.name: Invaded Barracks
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once the barracks of the Almuj Army, it has been taken over by the traitorous rebel, Commander Takan.

worldDiscovery.name: Iron Golem Factory
worldDiscovery.description: §7The source of the Iron Golems protecting Wynn and Gavel, and the methods of creating them, have remained unknown to the public eye.

worldDiscovery.name: Isles of Fiction
worldDiscovery.description: §7Previously only an empty stretch of ocean, this strange set of islands suddenly appeared to fill the space, seemingly out of nowhere. A strange magic fills the air.

worldDiscovery.name: Jofash Docks
worldDiscovery.description: §7A port town filled with odd residents and drunken sailors, Jofash Docks is the only entryway to Gavel outside of the main gate.

worldDiscovery.name: Jofash Tunnel
worldDiscovery.description: §7This dark tunnel connects the Sky Islands of Gavel to the Jofash Docks on the province's eastern coast, for those who wish to go sailing.

worldDiscovery.name: Kandon-Beda
worldDiscovery.description: §7A town built on the edge of a cliff, Kandon-Beda specializes in air travel. It also houses a library for those who wish to learn history.

worldDiscovery.name: Karoc Quarry
worldDiscovery.description: §7Karoc Quarry mines out massive amounts of quartz each day, mostly used for construction purposes. But lately, shipments have been going missing...

worldDiscovery.name: Katoa Ranch
worldDiscovery.description: §7This farm is most famous for its milk, though recently the cows that produce it have been going missing.

worldDiscovery.name: Kitrios Barracks
worldDiscovery.description: §7These barracks are where the Cinfras Army is positioned, though lately their General has been acting suspicious...

worldDiscovery.name: Lake Gylia
worldDiscovery.description: §7Named after the lush plains preceding it, Lake Gylia is the largest lake in the world. Immense magical energy surrounds this place.

worldDiscovery.name: Lazarus Pit
worldDiscovery.description: §7Gavel's counterpart to Wynn's Fountain of Youth, the Lazarus Pit has been resurrecting the dead inmates of the nearby prison.

worldDiscovery.name: Legendary Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home of the Legendary Challenge, a test for those who think that they are the strongest in the world. It is named for its designer, the eccentric Dr. Legendary.

worldDiscovery.name: Letvus Airbase
worldDiscovery.description: §7Letvus Airbase is the best place to go for commercial airship travel, though only the wealthiest can afford it.

worldDiscovery.name: Lexdale
worldDiscovery.description: §7This slumlike town has suffered the worst of the Decay's effects over the years, after its residents were forced out of their original homes by an unfortunate event.

worldDiscovery.name: Lexdale Penitentiary
worldDiscovery.description: §7Feared as the highest security prison in all of Gavel, this towering stronghold has held the worst criminals within its walls for centuries. In recent years, however, rumors have spread that they have begun arresting innocent citizens...

worldDiscovery.name: Light Forest Gate
worldDiscovery.description: §7This serves as the gateway to the brilliant Light Forest. Unlike the Dark Forest, this area hasn't been afflicted by the Decay, and remains as beautiful as ever.

worldDiscovery.name: Llevigar
worldDiscovery.description: §7The city of Llevigar is the first sight adventurers see in Gavel. It was built using quartz mined from the nearby Karoc Quarry. It is the most advanced city in Gavel.

worldDiscovery.name: Lusuco
worldDiscovery.description: §7In even the harshest of cold, the people of Lusuco have stayed put and continued living here.

worldDiscovery.name: Maex
worldDiscovery.description: §7A dwarven town founded as a monument to the victory in Molten Heights. It was built by the power of the people. Dwarves are communal and riches are owned by all.

worldDiscovery.name: Mage Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home to the reclusive mages responsible for calming the waves, the people on this island continue to study magic and advance their capabilities beyond any other.

worldDiscovery.name: Magmastream Core
worldDiscovery.description: §7Leading to the very heart of the tallest Volcano Isle, this tunnel almost feels alive- Magically-charged lava flows like a bloodstream around you. Small crystals are visible within the lavastreams on occasion.

worldDiscovery.name: Maltic
worldDiscovery.description: §7The first town founded by Villagers in the Wynn province. Maltic's beach is the site of a shipwreck that crashed upon its shores 100 years ago.

worldDiscovery.name: Maro Peaks
worldDiscovery.description: §7Proposals to colonise Maro Peaks have long been brought up as it holds great defensive and industrial benefits, but the local animal population proves impossible to work with. It is said Bob was the first man to climb to the peak.

worldDiscovery.name: Molten Heights Gate
worldDiscovery.description: §7The entryway to the Molten Heights, a fiery den of volcanoes. It's the home of the Dwarves, and the last remaining Doguns.

worldDiscovery.name: Monument to Siegfried
worldDiscovery.description: §7Built by the most skilled sculptors in Gavel, no expense was spared in constructing this enormous monument to Gavel's hero.

worldDiscovery.name: Nemract
worldDiscovery.description: §7Nemract is an under-defended, rough, seafaring city. The people here have endured a lot of suffering - and that has made them hardy.

worldDiscovery.name: Nesaak
worldDiscovery.description: §7In the center of the unnaturally frozen forest lies the small town of Nesaak. Their main source of food is ice fishing.

worldDiscovery.name: Nest of the Grootslangs
worldDiscovery.description: §7Just beyond this now abandoned ruin lies a dug out nest crawling with infantile Grootslangs. If left to mature, these newborn creatures will grow powerful enough to decimate the swamp.

worldDiscovery.name: Nivla Woods
worldDiscovery.description: §7This forest has been infested with giant, mutant arachnids for countless years. They serve as the greatest obstacle for adventurers on their way to Detlas.

worldDiscovery.name: North Lusuco
worldDiscovery.description: §7High up in the mountains, this area serves as the site of Lusuco's annual Wynnter Fair.

worldDiscovery.name: Olmic Cathedral
worldDiscovery.description: §7This old cathedral has fallen into disrepair, especially considering the massive spike that's grown inside of it. Legends say that beneath the cathedral lies a powerful rune.

worldDiscovery.name: Olux
worldDiscovery.description: §7Olux is a poor town, one that has suffered the effects of the Decay, a force that has caused all of north-western Gavel to begin to wither.

worldDiscovery.name: Olux Lumber Yard
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once used for repairs for Olux's massive wall, the lumber yard has become useless after the Decay began to make the trees rot and die.

worldDiscovery.name: Orphion's Nexus of Light
worldDiscovery.description: §7Thought only to be a myth, this legendary structure acts as a nexus for the Realm of Light's power. Somewhere within its enigmatic halls lies Orphion, the physical manifestation of Light itself.

worldDiscovery.name: Owl Tribe
worldDiscovery.description: §7The Owl Tribe is one of the two native tribes of the Rymek Canyon. They have been at war with the Eagle Tribe for years.

worldDiscovery.name: Ozoth's Spire
worldDiscovery.description: §7Tainted by the flames, this spire serves as the dramatic trail up to the nest of the dragon herself, Ozoth.

worldDiscovery.name: Pigmen's Ravine
worldDiscovery.description: §7This massive, maze-like ravine is home to the Pigmen, a species of half-pig, half-human abominations that feast on any weak adventurer who happens to stumble inside.

worldDiscovery.name: Pirate Cove
worldDiscovery.description: §7Founded by the first seafarer Redbeard, Pirate Cove is home to some of the most dangerous men in the ocean. They set up outposts all around the island to seek out merchant ships to plunder.

worldDiscovery.name: Pirate's Bay
worldDiscovery.description: §7A massive pirate ship crashed here with such severity, the hull was moulded into the cliffside. Some say that there is still treasure hidden inside.

worldDiscovery.name: Plague Laboratory
worldDiscovery.description: §7Mold, viscera, chemicals, and waste combine to give this laboratory a disgusting smell. Filth seems to cover every inch of this place.

worldDiscovery.name: Portal to Dern
worldDiscovery.description: §7Thought to be only a myth, this portal's existence confirms the legends that the Silent Expanse is a road to Dern. How to access it, though, is perhaps a mystery that can't be solved yet...

worldDiscovery.name: Prison of Souls
worldDiscovery.description: §7Oppressively dark and enigmatic, simply being inside this cave makes you feel disconnected from your body. Horrific power permeates the area...

worldDiscovery.name: Quartz Mansion
worldDiscovery.description: §7A group of bandits who have been stealing quartz shipments hide out in this mansion. However, no one knows what they're using it for.

worldDiscovery.name: Ragni
worldDiscovery.description: §7One of two great fortresses in Wynn. Ragni has survived over 1000 years of war - sometimes barely. It is the hometown of the 146 year old hero Bob, who hasn't been seen in 16 years.

worldDiscovery.name: Raiders' Port
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home of the Sky Raiders, a group of pirates who plunder and thieve through the air.

worldDiscovery.name: Relos
worldDiscovery.description: §7This coastal town is sprawling with gears and machinery built by the various engineers and mechanics living there. Importing and exporting take place frequently here.

worldDiscovery.name: River Sage
worldDiscovery.description: §7This is the widest river in Gavel, and stretches so long that it divides the plains in two.

worldDiscovery.name: Rodoroc
worldDiscovery.description: §7The capital of the Dwarven dominated Molten Heights. Carved from the rocks by the power of the people, it stands as one of the strongest cities ever made.

worldDiscovery.name: Rooster Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7This egg-shaped island is home to a single resident, a featherbrained villager hermit obsessed over chickens. Rumor has it that Bob has visited the island for some reason.

worldDiscovery.name: Roots of Corruption
worldDiscovery.description: §7A thousand years ago, a group of emerald miners unearthed a mysterious portal. Unknowingly, they doomed the province to centuries of war against the mysterious force known as corruption.

worldDiscovery.name: Rotten Passage
worldDiscovery.description: §7This tunnel seems to have been dug out unnaturally. The groans of the undead echo and clash in the fetid cavern air.

worldDiscovery.name: Ruined Olmic City
worldDiscovery.description: §7Though its creators have disappeared, this city serves as proof that there was once a civilization in the Silent Expanse.

worldDiscovery.name: Rymek
worldDiscovery.description: §7The town of Rymek was founded by the Creden Tibus bandit group. They only deal exchanges through a special currency.

worldDiscovery.name: Sanctuary Bridge
worldDiscovery.description: §7This magnificent bridge connects the mysterious ruins of Time Valley to the frigid cold of the Nesaak Tundra. In the center lies a mysterious seal that no one has been able to open.

worldDiscovery.name: Savannah
worldDiscovery.description: §7Wild ocelots inhabit this mostly-barren savannah, which leads to the Almuj desert. There is a town on the edge of the mountains, though few visit it.

worldDiscovery.name: Seavale Reef
worldDiscovery.description: §7Ocean currents flow and intertwine to form these renowned reefs, bursting with life. Tourists frequently dive and discover lost treasure, dropped from merchant ships and carried by the currents.

worldDiscovery.name: Selchar
worldDiscovery.description: §7Founded in 970 AP after the waves calmed, this city became the bridge between Gavel and Wynn. It flourishes in trade and enjoys a safe haven from the dangers of both provinces.

worldDiscovery.name: Silverbull Company Headquarters
worldDiscovery.description: §7Previously a summer residence for the Gavellian Monarchy, it had fallen into disrepair in the years since the fall of the Royalty. Several years ago, it was bought out and renovated by the Silverbull Trading Company to serve as their headquarters for business expansion.

worldDiscovery.name: Skiens Island
worldDiscovery.description: §7Held down by a fanatical military leader, their efforts prevented the colonization of Selchar for years. Somehow, the symptoms of corruption have begun, despite its position in the sea.

worldDiscovery.name: Soosu's Mansion
worldDiscovery.description: §7The seldom-seen Sovereign of the Roughworks, Soosu, is known to almost always be huddled up in this haphazard tower. What she does in there is anyone's guess.

worldDiscovery.name: Sunrise Canyon
worldDiscovery.description: §7A secluded gorge, rumored to be the territory of a notorious lone bandit. The stones turn a fiery spectrum of colors in the sunrise and set.

worldDiscovery.name: Taproot
worldDiscovery.description: §7In the midst of Decay, a massive amount of light is emanating from this root.

worldDiscovery.name: Temple of Legends
worldDiscovery.description: §7Only the most revered of scholars and adventurers are allowed inside of the Temple of Legends. Just entering this place is a testament to your victory against the Corrupter of Worlds.

worldDiscovery.name: Ternaves
worldDiscovery.description: §7This small town has laid quietly near Detlas for decades. Nearby lies a massive prison that few draw near.

worldDiscovery.name: Thanos
worldDiscovery.description: §7The only residence of dwarves outside of the Molten Heights, Thanos has been threatened by Ozoth, a powerful dragon feared across the entire province.

worldDiscovery.name: The Canopy's Hideout
worldDiscovery.description: §7With little government assistance, the people of the Mistwoods depend on various clans to keep them safe from the forest's mysterious threats. One such clan is The Canopy.

worldDiscovery.name: The Canyon Colossus
worldDiscovery.description: §7A sight to behold, this enormous statue has resided in the Canyon of the Lost for as long as history can tell. Its magic permeates throughout the entire canyon, making it nigh-impossible to navigate.

worldDiscovery.name: The Eldritch Outlook
worldDiscovery.description: §7It is impossible to tell the origins of this peculiar structure, but it might be the tallest in the known world. Climbing to the top could provide a great view.

worldDiscovery.name: The Forgery
worldDiscovery.description: §7While this may look like a simple bridge on the way to Cinfras, above it floats The Forgery, the gateway to Corrupted Dungeons.

worldDiscovery.name: The Frog Bog
worldDiscovery.description: §7The water that once circulated Auburn has long since stagnated. The lack of drainage to the ocean has caused a freshwater swamp to slowly develop in this corner of the forest. Spooky stories about what lies within the bog have led to it being an unfavourable place to live.

worldDiscovery.name: The Gateway to Light
worldDiscovery.description: §7Secluded beneath the remains of a colossal tree, this bizarre structure acts as a gateway to the Realm of Light, an otherworldly realm beyond imagination.

worldDiscovery.name: The Great Bridge
worldDiscovery.description: §7While it is unknown when it was built, this bridge serves as one of the only ways to access the Jungle. Usually the bustling of travelers and salesmen alike echoes throughout its halls...

worldDiscovery.name: The Guild Hall
worldDiscovery.description: §7The Guild Hall is where the most prestigious guilds gather, and where you can create your own. You can also get to Letvus Airbase through here.

worldDiscovery.name: The Highlands
worldDiscovery.description: §7Ever in bloom, the Highlands are home to those in Fruma who are fortunate enough to be a part of the upper-class. Lavish mansions inhabited by the aristocracy litter the region's gorgeous hills.

worldDiscovery.name: The House of Twain
worldDiscovery.description: §7Once the home of the most gifted defenders of Wynn, it now lies in ruins and haunted by the spirits that could not move on.

worldDiscovery.name: The Industrial District
worldDiscovery.description: §7Known to its inhabitants as the Roughworks, this region of the Province is devoted to the manufacture of products for richer areas. Its citizens toil endlessly, rewarded with a mere pittance - hardly enough to survive.

worldDiscovery.name: The Mistwoods
worldDiscovery.description: §7A dense fog permanently covers this vast redwood forest, meaning no outsider can see any more than just the towering treetops. As such, few know what goes on within the woods, which citizens are barred from entering.

worldDiscovery.name: The Moon
worldDiscovery.description: §7It seems the wizard's gravity spell made you fall all the way through space, landing on the moon itself. Weird.

worldDiscovery.name: The Outer Void
worldDiscovery.description: §7A strange aura emanating from a forgotten refuse within the Void has warped the gravity surrounding it, pulling in all sorts of fallen debris. An opportunity to exploit for some, a dangerous omen for others.

worldDiscovery.name: The Palace
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home to the Queen, this vast palace is the very heart of Fruma. It is filled with luxuries for Her Majesty to enjoy, for just like the province itself, it exists only to please her.

worldDiscovery.name: The Qira Hive
worldDiscovery.description: §7This towering nest is the Qira Hive, a challenge against the powerful creations of the enigmatic Qira.

worldDiscovery.name: The Royal Dam
worldDiscovery.description: §7This towering Dam restricts the flow of water from the Highlands into the Auburn Forest. The Crown claims it is to protect the citizens.

worldDiscovery.name: The Royal University of Aelumia
worldDiscovery.description: §7Colloquially known as Aelumia University, it is the most prestigious educational institution in Fruma. Many of the province's greatest minds have studied here, some later going on to become Sovereigns.

worldDiscovery.name: The Shiar
worldDiscovery.description: §7A village of hobbits, it is home to a blacksmith by the name of Dodegar, seeking to forge the ultimate weapon.

worldDiscovery.name: The Steel Feather
worldDiscovery.description: §7In this abandoned subway station, a group of citizens wronged by the government have gathered, building their own community beneath the eyes of the Crown.

worldDiscovery.name: The Void
worldDiscovery.description: §7A thousand meters beneath the Sky Islands, the remains of a fractured land linger. Strange forces warp its inhabitants, molding them for survival in unfamiliar conditions.

worldDiscovery.name: The Wellspring of Eternity
worldDiscovery.description: §7Home to one of the most beautiful gardens in Fruma, this royal bathhouse is off-limits to everyone but its staff, the Queen, and her Sovereigns - and as such, is guarded quite heavily. One must wonder what makes it so special...

worldDiscovery.name: Thesead
worldDiscovery.description: §7Thesead is one of the more isolated cities of Gavel, but its coal industry has kept it thriving. However, recently the coal mines have been ridden with an unusual gas.

worldDiscovery.name: Timasca
worldDiscovery.description: §7Although it is the most urban area of the Roughworks, the bar is not very high. The city is constantly growing and redeveloping, unmindful of what came before, resulting in its ramshackle state.

worldDiscovery.name: Time Valley
worldDiscovery.description: §7The ruins here have been present longer than the portal. Only one person alive today understands this strange place. Time acts strangely here and it's apparent that whoever made them were not human.

worldDiscovery.name: Tower of Ascension
worldDiscovery.description: §7The tallest tower in the province, the Tower of Ascension provides a challenge so daunting that it has brought down even the strongest of warriors.

worldDiscovery.name: Town of Lutho
worldDiscovery.description: §7A sight to behold, this eccentric town's glowing obelisk is a beacon of hope in the midst of the Silent Expanse.

worldDiscovery.name: Toxic Wastes
worldDiscovery.description: §7An area unlike any other, the green slime seems to move in symphony across the land. The unbearable smell and acidity makes it almost impossible to live in.

worldDiscovery.name: Tribal Sanctuary
worldDiscovery.description: §7This disused Iboju burial site seems to have been turned into a camp for the risen tribal zombies. Foreign chanting is a constant ambience here.

worldDiscovery.name: Troms
worldDiscovery.description: §7This massive fortress has served as a symbol of hope to the province throughout the war. Its army has helped keep the corruption at bay for centuries.

worldDiscovery.name: Troms Castle
worldDiscovery.description: §7The royal family of Troms has been the talk of the town lately. The king's son has gone missing, while the King himself remains reclusive and paranoid...

worldDiscovery.name: Upper Molten Heights
worldDiscovery.description: §7The volcanoes stand upon a hollow victory and support the few survivors of the war. Some argue it was a genocide, not a war.

worldDiscovery.name: Void Valley
worldDiscovery.description: §7This valley has been overgrown with gigantic plant life. Rumors suggest that small rifts in the ground allow one to teleport.

worldDiscovery.name: Void Village

cave.name: The Flush
cave.description: §7Strange voices can be heard coming from the drains in Timasca. The sewer entrance at §f[-1754, 20, -490]§7 may be worth investigating.

cave.name: The Gwanari
cave.description: §7Nothing escapes its maw. Never enter. Do not go near §f[983, 76, -470]§7 or you will be nothing but gristle and bone

cave.name: The Heartwood
cave.description: §7At §f[-625, 67, -289]§7 you will find heartwood and roots of a tree that once was

cave.name: The Lantern Keeper's Abode
cave.description: §7It burns itself alive to let others fade to ashes. The flames will burn you at §f[1285, 104, -1060]

cave.name: The Place Coalesced
cave.description: §7Wanderers out of Lutho are taken and changed at §f[879, 80, -803]§7, where sickness and toxicity are coalesced

cave.name: The Place Condensed
cave.description: §7Condensed and warped into new forms, new matter threatens the east of Lutho §f[1038, 85, -692]

cave.name: The Place Conglomerated
cave.description: §7In a place stolen by nothingness, life is conglomerated, mutated, and sent to grow over the stolen world from §f[1313, 146, -1048]

cave.name: The Sacrificial Altar
cave.description: §7Dark Cultists have gathered around an Altar in a cliffside cave around §f[-298, 39, -969]

cave.name: The Warehouse
cave.description: §7Bots manage a complex warehouse deep in the ground at §f[-1847, 42, -2641]

cave.name: Theatre Royal
cave.description: §7The dazzling Showman captivates crowds and influences opinions in the theatre at §f[-1763, 136, -798]

cave.name: Thunder Grub Nest
cave.description: §7A nest of thunderous grubs can be found around §f[-618, 64, -728]

cave.name: Tidepool Terrace
cave.description: §7Some trouble is brewing in some underground tidepools at §f[-512, 36, -2015]

cave.name: Time Valley Aquifer
cave.description: §7Some dangerous fauna can be found in this aquifer at §f[-377, 75, -1167]

cave.name: Toads in a Hole
cave.description: §7Toads have gathered together and made a huge den at §f[-1756, 58, -5133]

cave.name: Treasure Tomb
cave.description: §7A hidden tomb with a spiny guard around §f[1030, 69, -2169]

cave.name: Tree of Light
cave.description: §7Many mysteries and creatures make their home in the great tree at §f[-893, 55, -6217]

cave.name: Troll Burrows
cave.description: §7A large gathering of trolls are peacefully minding their business a bit too close to Thesead at §f[858, 81, -5198]

cave.name: Troll Tower
cave.description: §7Climb the elusive troll tower at §f[1106, 86, -2870]

cave.name: Trunk Garrison
cave.description: §7A garrison of the corrupt has taken refuge through a tree trunk at §f[-560, 80, -959]

cave.name: Visceral Remnant
cave.description: §7Bodies subjected to grisly stitching and experimentation rejuvenated and rise at §f[-997, 46, -5559]§7, where no one should enter

cave.name: Volcanus Pit
cave.description: §7Enter the depths of the Volcanic Isles at §f[-830, 41, -3794]

cave.name: Voltaic Crag
cave.description: §7Thunder elementals have carved a thunderbolt-shaped crag in the cliff at §f[632, 47, -4947]§7, and...?

cave.name: Vortex Complex
cave.description: §7Enter this deep and confusing complex and find what treasure lies within §f[-182, 73, -1210]

cave.name: Water Tower
cave.description: §7The government advises citizens to stay away from the Water Tower at §f[-2121, 61, -802]§7, but since when have you been following their rules?

cave.name: Watery Waterfall
cave.description: §7Animated water beings have formed in an odd cave nearby §f[536, 31, -5363]

cave.name: Wave Cult Temple
cave.description: §7Warlocks leading a sinister following can be found at §f[-251, 37, -2725]§7, preaching their tidal glory

cave.name: Weapons Factory
cave.description: §7The weapons factory at §f[-2187, 36, -751]§7 is heavily guarded... Perhaps it contains valuable loot.

cave.name: Weird Wilds
cave.description: §7As fate ordained, particularly strong Weirds have conglomerated at §f[-967, 66, -4353]§7 for purposes incomprehensible.

cave.name: Weirding Ring
cave.description: §7Instead of fae spirits, Weirds have congealed in a ring of giant climbable mushrooms at §f[-498, 42, -5293]

cave.name: Willow Crypt
cave.description: §7An ancient Crypt with hidden dangers can be found at §f[591, 82, -1866]

cave.name: Windwalker Temple
cave.description: §7A large tree has grown out of the hillside at §f[1358, 106, -4743]

cave.name: Winery Infestation
cave.description: §7An old wine cellar infested with the corrupt at §f[282, 71, -1392]

cave.name: Wolf Den
cave.description: §7The home of the wolves all around the frozen area of Nesaak, found around §f[175, 73, -715]

raid.name: Nest of the Grootslangs
raid.description: §7Homes ravaged by Decay, the subterranean Grootslangs search frantically for a better habitat. Though they harbor no ill intent, they pose a significant risk for the people of Olux, and perhaps for all of Gavel.

raid.name: Orphion's Nexus of Light
raid.description: §7The source of the Decay tearing apart Gavel lies within the great Nexus at the heart of the Realm of Light. The Light itself is fading. Its embodiment is suffering. Save Him.

raid.name: The Canyon Colossus
raid.description: §7In its effort to protect Gavel, the Canyon Colossus instead only endangers the province. With nobody to maintain it for centuries, it is on the verge of tearing the land asunder. It must be calmed, as soon as possible.

raid.name: The Nameless Anomaly
raid.description: §7Void Holes are strange... Are they natural? Or are they artificial? What makes them? What happens to the matter which they replace? A particularly large Void Hole rests within the centre of the Silent Expanse, perhaps it holds the answer to these questions...

bossAltar.name: Aerie of the Recluse
bossAltar.description: §7The Avos have exiled those who utilize Corkian technology. For some, it isn't a choice to use it or not. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Altar of Sanctification
bossAltar.description: §7Hostile spirits must be exorcised by force. Someone long lost to madness and paranoia would be a long struggle. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Arena of the Legends
bossAltar.description: §7The Temple of the Legends' soldiers undergo the most rigorous training in all of Wynn, and the results speak for themselves. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Bottomless Pit
bossAltar.description: §7How many old artifacts have fallen into the places where the earth opened up? Trying to enter those depths is a suicide mission. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Bovine Barn
bossAltar.description: §7Most people don't think Bovemists revere cows for a good reason. The people around here know better, and leave offerings regularly. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Challenge of the Blades
bossAltar.description: §7Things that lurk in the dark still hold fears. There's no rule that says the rulers of night can't turn on each other for power. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Deserter's Refuge
bossAltar.description: §7Those in the highest positions of Her Majesty's Guard may get peeks through the cracks in their worldview, but only the strongest among them are capable of the introspection needed to break through. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Geyser Pit
bossAltar.description: §7Despermechs have a nasty tendency to get into any place they can find. Plenty have dug too deep and destroyed themselves. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Magmastream Core
bossAltar.description: §7Powerful sources of magic change their surroundings in unexpected ways. In some cases, it can feel like the land is a vessel for the 'life' of magic. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Plague Laboratory
bossAltar.description: §7Immortality is a tricky aspect to distill, so it's best to start small. Malleable subjects make it easier. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Prison of Souls
bossAltar.description: §7The Twains' efforts in settling spirits wasn't without risk. Spiritual bonds break without someone to maintain them. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Rotten Passage
bossAltar.description: §7Corrupteds and undead rarely present a unified front. Whenever they do, it's usually evidence of a controlling force. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Sunrise Canyon
bossAltar.description: §7The Creden Tibus are disorganized enough that not all bandits ascribe to their rule, but not many of them are skilled enough to make it on their own. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Tribal Sanctuary
bossAltar.description: §7Even leaders revered for their accomplishments shouldn't be propped up as perfect. The Iboju people know this well now. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard

bossAltar.name: Unknown Area
bossAltar.description: §7There is no information available about this region. 󐀎Difficulty: Very Hard
